we rely on sophisticated information technology systems, including... artificial intelligence (AI)-enabled tools and systems... highly integrated into our business, including our R&D efforts, our clinical and commercial manufacturing processes

AI systems may produce inaccurate or flawed outputs due to flawed algorithms, or insufficient and/or erroneous training data... We may also become vulnerable to operational disruptions if the AI technologies we use experience downtimes or are compromised by cyberattacks.

the EU Artificial Intelligence (AI) Act... establishes a risk-based framework governing the development, deployment and use of AI systems... High-risk AI systems are subject to stringent requirements such as mandatory risk assessments, technical documentation, bias mitigation

limitations: No quantified AI-specific dollar amounts, GPU counts, or capex figures appear in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 3 based on named infrastructure (dedicated CTO role, AI Governance Council, Amgen-built tools) without quantified scale.

Narrative concreteness scored 4 (named deployed use cases with operational detail) but no quantified business results (e.g., % improvement, cost savings) were disclosed in the excerpts, preventing a score of 5.

AI Governance Council and named oversight roles (Chief Compliance Officer, SVP Artificial Intelligence Data) plus specific regulatory compliance mechanisms (EU AI Act, NIS2) support a score of 5 for risk disclosure depth.
